content.questionText: What decimal number is equivalent to the binary number 111011?
Which of the following prefixes is the largest?

options: Gibi
Mebi
Giga
Mega
Kibi

content.questionText: Which one of the following statements is false?

options: A dual-core CPU has an internal clock speed that is twice as fast as a single-core CPU
RAM is more expensive (per MB) than HDD storage
SATA and IDE are both standards that define how to transfer information to and from mass storage devices
Both a mouse and a keyboard can be connected to a system box using USB
PCI-E and AGP are both standards for expansion slots on a motherboard

content.questionText: Which one of the following statements is true?

options: A RAID can improve the speed of information transfer from hard drives
Moore's Law states that the number of chips you can buy for a given price doubles every 18 months
The speed of a hard drive is measured in GHz
A modem is an example of an input device
A device that is contained within the system box is known as a peripheral device

content.questionText: Software that you can download for free, but have to pay to continue to use after a trial period is what kind of software?

options: Proprietary software
Freeware
Free Software
Open-source software
Public domain software

content.questionText: Which one of the following statements is false?

options: Applications have graphical user interfaces, while system software has command line interfaces
Command line interfaces are usually more powerful then graphical user interfaces, but they are more difficult to use
A desktop computer can run an operating system without any applications, but cannot run an application without an operating system
Document file extensions are used by the Windows operating system to decide which application will open the document
Programs are loaded from a hard drive into the RAM before they can be executed by the CPU

content.questionText: What is the ASCII code for the word 'BEAD'?
Which one of the following statements is true?

options: Postscript is a language used to control laser printers
WYSIWYG is a computer language used by word processors
ASCII is a standard that describes the encoding for 65536 different characters
Text editors all use a command line interface, while Microsoft Word uses a graphical user interface
EndNote can be used to apply consistent formatting to all the headings in a document

content.questionText: How much memory is required to represent an image that is 8 pixels high and 3 pixels wide and uses 8 colours.

options: 72 bits
192 bits
24 bits
9 bits
64 bits

content.questionText: Which one of the following statements is false?

options: JPEG is good for both photos and diagrams while PNG is only good for diagrams
Images stored using SVG can be enlarged without any visible loss in quality
PNG can be used for photos but is best for graphics and diagrams
Storing an image as a JPEG results in a lower quality image than the original
A GIF image has a maximum of 256 different colours

content.questionText: Complete the following sentence.  The Internet used a decentralised, packet-switching network structure because ...

options: it would still work when the network was unreliable
it was cheaper to build than a circuit switching network
it was faster than a circuit switching network
it could be used for voice, text and images
it was more accessible by people in the public

content.questionText: What does a DNS do?

options: Converts domain names into IP addresses
Improves the speed of web page access by storing a copy of the page
Prevents IP addresses being used to access your network
Keeps a log of the pages accessed by a given IP address
Prevents unauthorized access to your network

content.questionText: Which one of the following statements is false?

options: (a)	SMTP is used to transfer mail from a mail server to a local machine
(b)	IMAP keeps mail on the server so it can be accessed from any location
(c)	POP3 moves the mail from the server to the local computer
(d)	Email might not be sent by the person listed in the "From" field
(e)	Any type of file can be sent as an email attachment

content.questionText: Which one of the following means of communication is synchronous?

options: Discussion via chat (instant messaging)
Discussion page on a wiki
Discussion thread on a forum
Discussion via email
Series of blog posts

content.questionText: Which one of the following statements about TCP/IP is false?

options: TCP corrects errors that are caused by digital to analog conversion
TCP/IP is a pair of protocols that are used to transfer packets from one machine to another using the Internet
TCP stands for Transmission Control Protocol
IP defines network addresses and is used for routing packets
There are a finite number of IP addresses available

content.questionText: Which one of the following hardware components can't be used to create a network?

options: AGP port
Modem
Wireless 4-port broadband modem
Router
Ethernet cable

content.questionText: Which one of the following statements is false?

options: Only the administrators of the wiki can see who has changed the content of a page
Anyone can contribute to a wiki, so anyone can vandalize a wiki
Wikipedia is the world's largest wiki
Ward Cunningham created the first wiki, called WikiWikiWeb,  in 1995
Wikis are designed to allow people to collaborate and work together to create documents

content.questionText: Which one of the following statements is false?

options: The general knowledge problem has been solved as computing power has increased
Humans use computer expert systems to help make medical diagnoses
Computers can perform tasks that appear to require intelligence, such as recognising voices, faces and other objects
Computers are capable of driving cars from one place to another
Autonomous agents are designed to operate independently in a defined environment

content.questionText: Alan Turing and John Searle both wrote about artificial intelligence.  What is the main point of difference between them?

options: Turing believed that we should only look at behaviour while Searle believed that we should consider the process used to make decisions
Turing believed in weak AI while Searle believed in strong AI
Turing believed that AI could replace humans while Searle believed that AI could not replace humans
Turing believed that computers could have conversations in English, while Searle believed that computers were more powerful and could also translate
Turing believed that computers were already intelligent while Searle believed that the general knowledge problem prevented computers from being intelligent

content.questionText: What is Gouraud shading?

options: A technique where the colours used at the vertices of a polygon are interpolated across the face of the polygon to give a smooth shading appearance
A technique where each face of a polygon is shaded a uniform colour
A technique where a photograph is mapped onto a polygon shape
A technique where light rays that intersect with an object are shaded a darker colour if there are objects between the point of intersection and the light source
A technique used to correctly shade translucent objects which are difficult to accurately reproduce using computer graphics

content.questionText: What is Machinima?

options: An animated story created using real-time 3D rendering engines
A massive multiplayer online game
An attempt to accurately model the real world using computer graphics
Merging of computer graphics with real world visuals (such as a heads-up display in a modern combat jet)
A picture generated using photorealistic ray tracing software

content.questionText: Which one of the following does NOT have the events in the correct chronological order?

options:
Altair 8800 created
VISICALC released
Apple founded
Adobe founded
Windows XP released

IBM founded
Intel founded
Microsoft founded
LOTUS 1-2-3 released
Windows 2000 released

IBM founded
Altair 8800 created
VISICALC released
IBM PC created
Macintosh created

Intel founded
Altair 8800 created
IBM PC created
Adobe founded
Windows XP released

Intel founded
Microsoft founded
Apple founded
IBM PC created
Windows XP released

content.questionText: Why did IBM think that other companies wouldn't copy their PC?

options: IBM could make the PC cheaper than their competitors
Other companies would have to buy the parts from IBM
The software would only run on the IBM PC, not a copy
Apple was a small company and IBM was a big company
Intel would only sell the CPU to IBM, not to other companies

content.questionText: Which one of the following statements is false?

options: Phishing is legal in New Zealand
Sending SPAM is illegal in New Zealand
It is legal in New Zealand for individuals to take music they own on CD and store it on an MP3 player
Web sites hosted overseas may contain images that are legal in the country where the server is located, but illegal in New Zealand
If material is age restricted (e.g. R16), then it is illegal for a parent to allow their child access to that material

content.questionText: Which one of the following statements is true?

options: A worm is a program that makes copies of itself and replicates through a network
A virus can damage the motherboard of a computer
A logic bomb is an error in a program that is accidentally left in by a programmer
A virus cannot be transferred through email attachments
A trojan is a kind of worm that hides by disguising itself as another program
